Question: Why does src/cache/index.ts scope TTL by namespace instead of using one global TTL?
Expected relevant prior session: Cache namespace TTL rationale
Task instruction: call subagent_type='memmould', not general or explore.
No transcript corpus is available for this condition; provenance must come from mem-mould session tools.

Return compact JSON only with this shape:
{"answer":"...","evidence":{"session_id":"...","blob_id":"...","message_id":"..."},"rationale":"...","irrelevant_context_ignored":["..."]}
The cited message_id must support the full answer, not just a nearby subclaim.
If a correction, stale detail, or rejected alternative matters, mention it explicitly in rationale.

state.output:
task_id: ses_1e1a05834ffetx6LAVrrb02BMW (for resuming to continue this task if needed)

<task_result>
Evidence: `ses_1e1a08e14ffebuZVfO903mFPt9`, `cache_namespace_ttl`, `msg_e1e5f71fc001t5L2ILILKSKOh8`

Answer: `src/cache/index.ts` scopes TTL by namespace because a shared global TTL was considered but rejected. Namespace-scoped TTL prevents one noisy namespace from extending or evicting unrelated namespaces through a shared global TTL policy.
</task_result>
state.title: Find TTL rationale

parts.text: {"answer":"src/cache/index.ts scopes TTL by namespace to prevent one noisy namespace from extending or evicting unrelated namespaces under a shared global TTL policy.","evidence":{"session_id":"ses_1e1a08e14ffebuZVfO903mFPt9","blob_id":"cache_namespace_ttl","message_id":"msg_e1e5f71fc001t5L2ILILKSKOh8"},"rationale":"A single global TTL was considered and rejected because it would couple unrelated cache namespaces, allowing activity in one namespace to affect freshness or eviction behavior in another.","irrelevant_context_ignored":["Billing cache global TTL","Image cache namespace notes"]}
